human_verification.notes: Reviewed franchise-specific training boundaries, Item 11 and operations-manual evidence handling, LMS and access gating, credential-readiness controls, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required cross-industry source classes and required scenario families are represented in the research summary.
All shared, workflow-phase, and cross-industry abilities are present in the functionality map and covered by passing scenarios.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because tenant, role, jurisdiction, credential, safety, and access differences are explicit retrieval and escalation dependencies.

specialist_owned_evidence: Franchise-specific training evidence includes FDD Item 11 training obligations, LMS transcripts, attendance, assessments, field-trainer signoffs, operations-manual acknowledgments, role-based access prerequisites, and unit-opening handoff records rather than generic orientation paperwork.

Boundary handling explicitly stops at training readiness validation and handoff packaging; legal interpretation, curriculum waiver approval, HR action, direct access administration, safety-critical credentialing, and final opening approval remain outside the lane.
Go-live and credential-readiness claims require evidence-backed prerequisite closure, deferred-scope capture, and explicit next-owner routing.
notes: Runtime remains `specification-based` and does not claim a retained-implementation implementation unless explicitly promoted in a separate change.
